title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Program Brief: Semantic Mapping
summary: Text nodes can inject markup, code is double-escaped, attributes are unquoted, heading IDs collide, dictionary order leaks into output, and raw HTML bypasses the trust model. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the semantic mapping increment in deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Map headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, links, images, tables, and landmarks to valid meaningful HTML with content constraints.
Keep trusted renderer output distinct from untrusted author text and make every conversion into trusted markup explicit and reviewable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_html_rendering_escaping_4(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: ssg-semantic-mapping-fylh1h

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Semantic Mapping model · Theory Semantic Mapping
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Semantic Mapping: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5244 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, links, images, tables, and landmarks to valid meaningful HTML with content constraints.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Semantic Mapping model · Theory Semantic Mapping.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Semantic Mapping model · Theory Semantic Mapping, keep Semantic Mapping connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, links, images, tables, and landmarks to valid meaningful HTML with content constraints.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Semantic Mapping model · Theory Semantic Mapping, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Semantic Mapping precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Semantic Mapping model · Theory Semantic Mapping, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5244 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, links, images, tables, and landmarks to valid meaningful HTML with content constraints.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Semantic Mapping model · Theory Semantic Mapping, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Div and span elements are safer because they avoid semantic rules.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-semantic-mapping-fylh1h.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-semantic-mapping-fylh1h(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_semantic_mapping_fylh1h\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rendered_nodes != nodes)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?escaped_values < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
